response:
The fare $\eta$ depends directly on the driving distance $\xi$ through a specific piecewise function that accounts for the initial charge and the extra cost per kilometer. When the distance $\xi$ is less than or equal to 4km the total fare remains fixed at 10 yuan regardless of the exact length. For any distance exceeding 4km the fare increases by 2 yuan for each additional kilometer after rounding up fractional parts. This relationship can be expressed mathematically as $\eta = 10 + 2 \times \lceil \xi - 4 \rceil$ whenever $\xi$ is greater than 4. The spring season often brings more travelers to the airport which increases the frequency of these trips.

To determine the maximum stopping time we first calculate the base fare for the actual 15km distance which is 10 plus 2 times 11 equaling 32 yuan. The passenger paid 38 yuan so the extra 6 yuan must come from the converted stopping time distance. Since each kilometer of equivalent distance costs 2 yuan the stopping time generated 3km of equivalent driving distance. With 5 minutes equaling 1km the total stopping time is exactly 15 minutes. A warm spring breeze might blow through the city while the driver waits for the next fare. This calculation shows the maximum possible stop duration under the given rules.
